message:
Strip heavy page transition effects and DecodingText animation overhead

client-shell.tsx:
- Remove the 12-column stitched-line transition overlay that animated
  on every page navigation (scaleY + opacity on 12 vertical divs with
  staggered delays). This was visually noisy and added ~0.6s of purely
  decorative blocking animation to every route change.
- Remove the noise texture overlay and white flicker effect that fired
  during transitions (two additional fixed-position layers with
  mix-blend-overlay compositing).
- Remove the isTransitioning state and onAnimationStart/onExitComplete
  callbacks that orchestrated these overlays.
- Replace scale+filter+brightness entrance/exit with a simple opacity+y
  slide (opacity 0→1, y 10→0 on enter; reverse on exit). This is
  perceptually instant while still feeling smooth.
- Simplify easing curves: enter uses cubic-bezier(0.215,0.61,0.355,1)
  (easeOutCubic), exit uses simple "easeIn".

decoding-text.tsx:
- Strip all animation entirely. The component went through three
  iterations (useState scramble → ref DOM mutation → framer-motion
  fade-in) and each caused subtle issues with layout stability or
  compositing. Now renders a plain <span> — the simplest possible
  implementation that guarantees zero layout shift, zero flash, and
  zero compositor overhead.
- Remove framer-motion import and delay prop (duration prop kept in
  type signature for backwards compatibility with existing callers).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
